title: SpaceX IPO quality model
question: Does the SpaceX IPO setup justify participating once free float, lock-ups, control, underwriters, index inclusion rules and high-quality IPO comps are screened honestly?
finding: Trade-only / wait-for-unlock bias. Combined with study 15, the prior is harsh: broad day-1-close IPO chasing had a -28.4% median one-year excess return versus SPY, and even the filtered large-IPO comp median is about -14.7% at six months from first close. SpaceX is exceptional on team, underwriting, scale and scarcity, but immediate public float is only about 4.25% of basic shares and potential tradable supply reaches about 5.24B shares by day 180, or roughly 9.4x the IPO float and 40% of basic shares. Class B keeps 88.5% voting power, Musk controls about 84.4%, and Nasdaq-100 fast entry can create early flow, but S&P 500 inclusion cannot under current rules because 12-month seasoning, profitability and IWF screens remain. Connectivity is the quality anchor; AI/xAI/X is the high-capex swing factor.
